item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
with no observable discrepancies other than the providing individual being a non-human entity comprised entirely of worms.

+1. Incredible work. Gave me a warm feeling inside. Fun to read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Only problem I spotted: it's the University of Nebraska at Kearney, not the University of Nebraska at Kearny.

Otherwise, good one. Nebraska indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He's got my vote. I think the foundation is a lil bit otherist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I edited a typo, and forgot to write a description of the change. I just changed an "an" to an "in" in the fourth paragraph of the description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll always maintain that "predictability" is not inherently bad, for two reasons:

  1. A predictable ending that, theoretically, could be subverted (and often isn't) can heighten audience enjoyment if there's sufficient engagement in the outcome.
  2. Predictability is too often used as a crutch against engagement. A reader should be enjoying their way through, rather than worrying how everything will be resolved.

This skip is perfectly predictable, but it's written so charmingly that I'd venture to say it's far more engaging than the majority of shit on TV.

+1

item.title: one of my favorite cad works i somehow forgot to upvote
item.content:encoded: Theres something about the name of this guy that i find so appealing. cant put my finger on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So what if he is? Come on seagull, I thought you were better than this ;P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, this skip has a special place in my heart. I'm originally from Nebraska, and it's good to see the cornhusker state getting some attention! Fun fact about the 3rd district of Nebraska, it was held by the University of Nebraska's football coach after he won us three national titles back in the 90s.

One thing I'd change is that he wouldn't be campaigning in Omaha. That's the second congressional district.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic work! Not to mention timely. Only issues would be that "bubble-headed dingleberries" is a bit too much to take seriously and that I'm very much not a fan of the banner and quad classification. Both are ultimately dwarfed by what this does right, however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"among a number of others, with no observable discrepancies other than the providing individual being a non-human entity comprised entirely of worms."

And with that, you wrote my favorite SCP. This is stellar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely ridiculous. There's no way an article like this belongs on a website full of existential horror, death, destruction, and Procedure 110-Montauk's.

+1 for making us a little less horrified and a little more giggly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is now canon

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My thoughts too! And I just imagined a little kid made of worms playing little league before a baseball flies right through his head making a big circular hole. A group of kids surround him for several seconds, nervously holding their breath, before he starts reforming and says "I'm okay!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My question is if they look at school photos of him, has bob always been worms?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This…that speech was done really well. Hell, I'd vote for him by the end of that.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But they're not relaxed! Every time they try to discredit him on his appearance, they're branded as racist, his politics are unimpeachable, and if they just try and restrain him or shoot him, he disappears in a puff of worms! He's a nightmare!

A nightmare I'd definitely vote for!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's keter because every time they try to contain it, it reappears back in the 3rd congressional district of Nebraska. From the box test, it's actively trying to get out of the box.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I for one welcome our first worm that walks congress member

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mean, I don't see why he's Keter. On the other hand-equivalent-made-of-worms, if Bob gets elected to Congress, can he even go to DC without discorporating?

If he can do it, though, I'd vote for him. I've had worse representation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I. What. why

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Early Worm gets the Bob. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No he isn't! A normal politician is a disgusting monster. Bob is just a humble pile of all-American worms

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has vibes from the "Worm that Walks" phenomenon in WJS Proposal, but takes it in its own unique direction deserving of a separate scip. The apparent dissonance between the guy apparently being a normal dude running for political office and obviously being a pile of wriggling purple worms is captured really really well, in both the speech and interview.

Makes me wonder if I should be liking him since he's so reasonable and otherwise normal, or disliking him because he's anomalous.

+ Side note
- Hide note

I'm not saying the article should change, but I wonder if containment would be possible if they contained his non-anomalous wife and kids, who presumably can't escape via disintegration. Maybe content for a follow-up tale?

+1, obviously

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4553 waves to its wife, Sandra Shepherd, 39, seated at the fore of the crowd with their two non-anomalous children. She laughs with the crowd, and waves back.

Whu-huhh… Wh-What…?

This line got me good. It's my favorite line in a really good overall piece. I wasn't expecting to be slammed by such a big wave of WTF while waiting patiently (and futilely) for Bob to show some kind of malevolence.

From this, I can only assume that Bob Shepherd wasn't always an animate mass of worms, but probably became such shortly before running for Congress. But even if this wasn't the case and he was born this way, I'd still smile and +1 while angrily screaming in confusion.

Nicely done. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this.

Honestly, he seems like a pretty good joe. Or good bob, as it were. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "There's no prob with Bob!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: cadaver commander, cantankerous creator of creepy crawlies crammed full of character and class

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd be down to vote for bob

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get what this scp's about. It's just a normal politician. Still, I'll give it a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cadaver writing another skip, a skip centered around politics actually being pretty good, next thing you know pigs will be flying loops in experimental spacecraft.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can’t believe everyone in this discussion page is saying “I’d vote for him tbh” about a literal pile of worms. It really speaks to how well the character is written1.

+1 for making a political SCP that isn’t too political

Footnotes
1. Also probably about the current state of the American political system.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe we should just have work people as politicians. Bob sure as hell has my vote.

item.title: Re: vote Bob Shepherd 2020
item.content:encoded: Can we get this guy to run for POTUS in a tale later?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: By the end of that speech *I* was ready to vote for him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted at the first line of the description, and went through the rest of the article wishing I could upvote 5-10 more times.

I actually think this might be one of my favorite recent SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so incredibly, ridiculously, inexcusably stupid. It makes no sense, the Foundation would not be this relaxed about an SCP involved in politics, it's unconscionable that this is upvoted.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: u just can’t beat em

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: BA-DUM-TSS

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, writing political skips just opens up a can o

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love political content.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fuck, I’d vote for him. Bob Shepherd for president.

item.title: vote Bob Shepherd 2020
item.content:encoded: make america squishy again
